How difficult is it for you to meditate?
What would happen if you simply refused to make a decision today?
cards.meaning_upright: Rest, recovery, retreat, stillness, and recharging.
cards.meaning_reversed: Burnout, restlessness, forced rest, returning to action, and exhaustion ignored.
cards.love: A pause to breathe and reflect rather than pushing the relationship's questions right now.
cards.career: Genuine downtime and recovery before performance suffers.
cards.spiritual: Rest as sacred practice, retreat, meditation, and silence as the soul's repair.
cards.waite_description: The effigy of a knight in the attitude of prayer, at full length upon his tomb.
cards.waite_divinatory: Vigilance, retreat, solitude, hermit's repose, exile, tomb and coffin. It is these last that have suggested the design.
cards.waite_reversed: Wise administration, circumspection, economy, avarice, precaution, testament.

cards.name: Five of Swords
fortune_telling: Someone is stealing from you, financially or romantically
Be wary of friends who talk behind your back

light: Acting in your own best interest
Choosing to stand up for yourself
Not backing down from disagreement and discord
Refusing to go along with an unethical plan
Knowing when to bend the rules
shadow: Taking advantage of others
Intimidating others
Acting in an unethical manner
Picking fights
Using words to goad others into violence and irrationality
Ignoring rules you've agreed to abide by
Looking out for yourself while allowing harm to come to others
Gloating over victory

cards.Affirmation: "Even as I care for myself, I am mindful of the needs of others."
Questions to Ask: How do I behave when I win? What does that say about me?
How can I do what's necessary without making others feel defeated?
What's the difference between selfish action and acting in my own best interest?
cards.meaning_upright: Hollow victory, conflict, winning at all costs, discord, and self-interest.
cards.meaning_reversed: Reconciliation, making amends, releasing resentment, lingering grudges, and lesson learned.
cards.love: Fights where winning the point loses the person, pride is the real opponent.
cards.career: Cutthroat dynamics, hollow wins, burned bridges, long-term costs to weigh.
cards.spiritual: Choosing peace over victory, releasing the need to be right in favor of what is whole.
cards.waite_description: A disdainful man looks after two retreating and dejected figures. Their swords lie upon the ground. He carries two others on his left shoulder, and a third sword is in his right hand, point to earth. He is the master in possession of the field.
cards.waite_divinatory: Degradation, destruction, revocation, infamy, dishonour, loss, with the variants and analogues of these.
cards.waite_reversed: The same; burial and obsequies.

cards.name: Six of Swords
fortune_telling: You'll soon go on a long journey over water
Actions have unexpected consequences, so be prepared

light: Making the best of a bad situation
Helping others who find themselves in dire circumstances
Changing the way you see the world
Broadening your perspective through study or travel
shadow: Refusing to accept that things have changed
Playing the victim
Rejecting the idea that your actions have consequences
Applying scientific criteria to matters of faith, or confusing faith with science
Believing the whole world should be like your small corner of it

cards.Affirmation: "I keep an open mind."
Questions to Ask: What assumptions govern my thinking?
How willing am I to lend aid to others? To request it when I need it?
How prepared am I to deal with change? With unexpected outcomes?
cards.meaning_upright: Transition, moving on, calmer waters, passage, and gradual healing.
cards.meaning_reversed: Resisting transition, unfinished business, rough crossing, returning to trouble, and stuck baggage.
cards.love: Moving past a rough patch together, or transitioning out of a relationship toward peace.
cards.career: A transition, new role, new environment, that trades turbulence for steadier progress.
cards.spiritual: Passage, carrying the lessons while letting the shore of suffering recede.
cards.waite_description: A ferryman carrying passengers in his punt to the further shore. The course is smooth, and seeing that the freight is light, it may be noted that the work is not beyond his strength.
cards.waite_divinatory: journey by water, route, way, envoy, commissionary, expedient.
cards.waite_reversed: Declaration, confession, publicity; one account says that it is a proposal of love.

cards.name: Seven of Swords
fortune_telling: Don't assume people around you are worthy of your trust
Ask for an accounting of where people have been, and what they've been doing

light: Refusing to do something dishonest, even when there's no chance of ever being caught
Handling a difficult situation with finesse
Acting ethically in public and in private
Living a life that is beyond reproach
shadow: Stealing or lying
Doing whatever you can get away with, simply because you can
Looking for a way around consequences
Justifying wicked behavior by focusing on the wickedness of others
Failing to examine your own motives and prejudices

cards.Affirmation: "I hold myself to the highest ethical standards."
Questions to Ask: What assumptions am I making?
How well-defined is my sense of ethics?
How should I respond when I know others are breaking the rules?
cards.meaning_upright: Deception, strategy, acting alone, getting away with it, and cunning.
cards.meaning_reversed: Confession, conscience, exposure, coming clean, and self-deception revealed.
cards.love: Hidden agendas, secrets, or avoidance of honest confrontation.
cards.career: Office politics, cut corners, uncredited ideas, work worth guarding.
cards.spiritual: Honesty with self, noticing where cleverness hides avoidance and choosing the direct path.
cards.waite_description: A man in the act of carrying away five swords rapidly; the two others of the card remain stuck in the ground. A camp is close at hand.
cards.waite_divinatory: Design, attempt, wish, hope, confidence; also quarrelling, a plan that may fail, annoyance. The design is uncertain in its import, because the significations are widely at variance with each other.
cards.waite_reversed: Good advice, counsel, instruction, slander, babbling.

cards.name: Eight of Swords
fortune_telling: Get over playing the victim
Once you realize you are your own biggest obstacle, nothing can hold you back

light: Deciding to go on a diet for your health's sake
Recognizing you cannot always be in control
Refusing to think about unhealthy or unethical options
shadow: Feeling trapped
Being lost in a maze of rules and regulations
Giving in to despair
Playing the victim
Allowing others to dictate what you can and cannot do
Being rendered helpless
Having very few options
Failing to look for a way out

cards.Affirmation: "I know my own limits."
Questions to Ask: Who's empowered to cut through the red tape?
What, exactly, are the obstacles? What resources, exactly, are needed to move them?
To what extent is your powerlessness a matter of attitude?
cards.meaning_upright: Feeling trapped, self-restriction, victim mindset, mental prison, and paralysis.
cards.meaning_reversed: Liberation, new perspective, self-empowerment, blindfold removed, and taking responsibility.
cards.love: Feeling stuck in a dynamic that beliefs, not circumstances, are maintaining.
cards.career: Feeling trapped in a role, the constraints deserve testing, most are assumptions.
cards.spiritual: Freeing the mind, seeing that most cages are beliefs, and beliefs can be set down.
cards.waite_description: A woman, bound and hoodwinked, with the swords of the card about her. Yet it is rather a card of temporary durance than of irretrievable bondage.
cards.waite_divinatory: Bad news, violent chagrin, crisis, censure, power in trammels, conflict, calumny; also sickness.
cards.waite_reversed: Disquiet, difficulty, opposition, accident, treachery; what is unforeseen; fatality.

cards.name: Nine of Swords
fortune_telling: If you take the action you're considering now, you'll be sorry in the future

light: Refusing to worry about what you cannot control
Judging your own performance with kindness and gentleness
Using meditation to quiet a troubled mind
Drawing a conclusion and putting an issue out of your mind
shadow: Torturing yourself with regrets
Second-guessing your every move
Beating yourself up for your mistakes
Depression
Obsessing on errors and overlooked details
Refusing to handle stress in healthy ways
Ruining your ability to appreciate the present by dwelling on the past
Debating irreversible decisions

cards.Affirmation: "I do not worry about what I cannot control."
Questions to Ask: What role does worry play in your current situation?
To what other ends might you devote the energy you're giving to anxiety?
How can you know when it's time to stop thinking and start acting?
cards.meaning_upright: Anxiety, sleepless nights, worry, dread, and mental anguish.
cards.meaning_reversed: Easing anxiety, facing fears, seeking help, perspective returning, and recovery from despair.
cards.love: Anxiety and worst-case spirals, fears best checked against reality.
cards.career: Stress and dread outpacing the actual situation, name the worry and it shrinks.
cards.spiritual: Meeting fear with compassion, naming the dread aloud so daylight can right-size it.
cards.waite_description: One seated on her couch in lamentation, with the swords over her. She is as one who knows no sorrow which is like unto hers. It is a card of utter desolation.
cards.waite_divinatory: Death, failure, miscarriage, delay, deception, disappointment, despair.
cards.waite_reversed: Imprisonment, suspicion, doubt, reasonable fear, shame.

cards.name: Ten of Swords
fortune_telling: Disaster
Put off plans and do not take action until omens are better

light: Seeing the signs that you've reached your limits
Paying attention to what your body is trying to tell you
Giving in to the need for rest and renewal
shadow: Accepting defeat prematurely
Driving yourself to total exhaustion, especially mentally
Experiencing a mental breakdown
Obsessing on a problem to the breaking point
Giving up
Refusing to move from thought to action
Deeply unhealthy thoughts

cards.Affirmation: "When my limits are exceeded, I take action on my own behalf."
Questions to Ask: What are the signs that the time for debate is over?
How can you tell when interest has given way to obsession?
When your own limits are reached, where can you turn for aid?
cards.meaning_upright: Painful ending, rock bottom, betrayal, collapse, and the worst is over.
cards.meaning_reversed: Recovery, rising again, surviving the ending, old wounds closing, and refusing the final lesson.
cards.love: A painful but final ending or betrayal, the closure, however harsh, frees what comes next.
cards.career: A project or role collapsing completely, salvage the lesson and rebuild elsewhere.
cards.spiritual: Surrender and rebirth, lying down with what is over so the sunrise can begin something new.
cards.waite_description: A prostrate figure, pierced by all the swords belonging to the card.
cards.waite_divinatory: Whatsoever is intimated by the design; also pain, affliction, tears, sadness, desolation. It is not especially a card of violent death.
cards.waite_reversed: Advantage, profit, success, favour, but none of these are permanent; also power and authority.

cards.name: Page of Swords
fortune_telling: This card represents a young man or woman with an airy, intellectual demeanor, likely born a Capricorn, Aquarius, or Pisces, who wants to learn something new from you or have a discussion with you

light: Investing time in study and practice
Making a habit of learning new things
Outlining what you need to know
shadow: Pretending to knowledge or sophistication you do not possess
Cheating on an exam
Feigning interest as a way of gaining favor
Considering only the evidence that supports conclusions you've already drawn
Rejecting the wise counsel of experienced teachers

cards.Affirmation: "I am ready to make good decisions."
Questions to Ask: How comfortable are you with revealing your own ignorance?
What are the marks of a good student?
To what extent are you open to new information?
cards.meaning_upright: Curiosity, vigilance, new ideas, mental energy, and truth-seeking.
cards.meaning_reversed: Gossip, hasty words, scattered thinking, spying, and all talk.
cards.love: Probing questions and honest talk, though reversed it warns of suspicion or gossip corroding trust.
cards.career: Research, due diligence, and fresh ideas, verify before broadcasting.
cards.spiritual: Truth-seeking, the watchful mind as a young guardian of honesty.
cards.waite_description: A lithe, active figure holds a sword upright in both hands, while in the act of swift walking. He is passing over rugged land, and about his way the clouds are collocated wildly. He is alert and lithe, looking this way and that, as if an expected enemy might appear at any moment.
cards.waite_divinatory: Authority, overseeing, secret service, vigilance, spying, examination, and the qualities thereto belonging.
cards.waite_reversed: More evil side of these qualities; what is unforeseen, unprepared state; sickness is also intimated.

cards.name: Knight of Swords
fortune_telling: A blunder leads someone to say something he or she regrets
If this was you, be prepared to apologize and move on

shadow: Stating your opinions as fact
Picking fights
Starting arguments
Using clever insults to undermine the confidence of others
Tossing reason out the window
Speaking without taking the feelings of others into account
Going on a witch hunt
Distorting evidence

cards.Affirmation: "I temper my insights with tact."
Questions to Ask: What do I really need to know?
To what extent have I investigated the facts behind my situation?
How can I share what I know without alienating others?
cards.meaning_upright: Decisive action, ambition, directness, charging ahead, and intellectual drive.
cards.meaning_reversed: Recklessness, aggression, rushing past details, burnout sprint, and verbal attacks.
cards.love: Intense, fast-moving pursuit, exciting but potentially inconsiderate of a partner's pace.
cards.career: Bold, rapid execution of a clear plan, guard against steamrolling colleagues.
cards.spiritual: Focus tempered, aiming the mind's speed so conviction doesn't outrun wisdom.
cards.waite_description: He is riding in full course, as if scattering his enemies. In the design he is really a prototypical hero of romantic chivalry. He might almost be Galahad, whose sword is swift and sure because he is clean of heart.
cards.waite_divinatory: Skill, bravery, capacity, defence, address, enmity, wrath, war, destruction, opposition, resistance, ruin. There is therefore a sense in which the card signifies death, but it carries this meaning only in its proximity to other cards of fatality.
cards.waite_reversed: Imprudence, incapacity, extravagance.

cards.name: Queen of Swords
fortune_telling: This card represents a woman with an artistic, intellectual nature, likely born between September 12th and October 12th, who uses clever, positive communication to sway others to her point of view

light: Knowing what to say and how to say it
Making others feel comfortable and confident
Bringing out the best in everyone
Possessing a knack for music, math, art, or science
shadow: Knowing exactly what to say to destroy another person
Withholding critical information
Using a barbed tongue to upset others
Employing sarcasm
Mimicking others unkindly
Making light of the less fortunate
Being disrespectful
Failing to use the talent you've been given

cards.Affirmation: "I make the truth easier to hear."
Questions to Ask: What do I have a knack for? How might my special gift prove useful now?
To what extent am I capable of saying what needs to be said?
What is the best possible way to say what I want to say?
cards.meaning_upright: Clear judgment, independence, honest communication, boundaries, and experienced wisdom.
cards.meaning_reversed: Coldness, bitterness, harsh criticism, isolation, and cynicism.
cards.love: Honesty and independence, genuine connection must respect her boundaries and intelligence.
cards.career: Sharp judgment and fair, direct leadership, say the true thing kindly.
cards.spiritual: Discernment, truth with compassion, boundaries held with grace.
cards.waite_description: Her right hand raises the weapon vertically and the hilt rests on an arm of her royal chair the left hand is extended, the arm raised her countenance is severe but chastened; it suggests familiarity with sorrow. It does not represent mercy, and, her sword notwithstanding, she is scarcely a symbol of power.
cards.waite_divinatory: Widowhood, female sadness and embarrassment, absence, sterility, mourning, privation, separation.
cards.waite_reversed: Malice, bigotry, artifice, prudery, bale, deceit.

cards.name: King of Swords
fortune_telling: This card represents an older man with an insightful, deliberate spirit, likely born between May 11th and June 10th, who is known for his integrity and sharp decision-making ability

light: Expressing yourself with firmness and authority
Reaching a beneficial agreement based on sound information
shadow: Insisting on having the last word
Flaunting your intellectual capability
Talking "over the heads" of others
Waffling on an important decision
Constantly changing your mind
Refusing to make choices that are in your own best interest
Wishing in vain you could take back what's been said

cards.Affirmation: "My word is my bond."
Questions to Ask: What would your decision be if you had to render a binding verdict right now?
How comfortable are you saying exactly what you mean? How often do you temper what you have to say for fear of offending others?
If you were to ask others, "What's my area of expertise?" what would they say?
cards.meaning_upright: Intellectual authority, truth, impartial judgment, strategy, and ethical clarity.
cards.meaning_reversed: Abuse of intellect, cold ruthlessness, manipulation, rigid logic, and unjust judgment.
cards.love: Rational steadiness that must remember to speak the heart's language too.
cards.career: Authoritative expertise, legal or strategic matters, and decisions that must be scrupulously fair.
cards.spiritual: The ethical mind, clarity serving fairness, intellect answering to principle.
cards.waite_description: He sits in judgment, holding the unsheathed sign of his suit. He recalls, of course, the conventional Symbol of justice in the Trumps Major, and he may represent this virtue, but he is rather the power of life and death, in virtue of his office.
cards.waite_divinatory: Whatsoever arises out of the idea of judgment and all its connexions-power, command, authority, militant intelligence, law, offices of the crown, and so forth.
cards.waite_reversed: Cruelty, perversity, barbarity, perfidy, evil intention.

cards.name: Ace of Wands
fortune_telling: Someone has the "hots" for you
A new job offer is coming your way
Walk softly, and carry a big stick

light: Being given the opportunity to do whatever you want to do
shadow: Failing to take advantage of a great opportunity
Being ineffectual or lazy
Making an inadequate effort
Working toward a goal, but lacking the resources or initiative to achieve success
Setting inappropriate goals
Failing to take a stand

cards.Numerology: 1 (The Origin: the starting point, the seed, opportunity)
cards.Affirmation: "I jump at the opportunity to pursue my heart's desire."

What happens if I let this opportunity pass me by?
How clearly have I defined my directions, values, and goals?
cards.meaning_upright: Inspiration, new opportunity, creative spark, enthusiasm, and potential.
cards.meaning_reversed: Delays, false start, lack of motivation, blocked creativity, and hesitation.
cards.love: A spark of fresh attraction or renewed passion in an existing bond.
cards.career: A new project or venture, strong potential if acted on quickly.
cards.spiritual: The creative spark as gift, inspiration from beyond, a call to act.
cards.waite_description: A hand issuing from a cloud grasps a stout wand or club.
cards.waite_divinatory: Creation, invention, enterprise, the powers which result in these; principle, beginning, source; birth, family, origin, and in a sense the virility which is behind them; the starting point of enterprises; according to another account, money, fortune, inheritance.
cards.waite_reversed: Fall, decadence, ruin, perdition, to perish also a certain clouded joy.

cards.name: Two of Wands
fortune_telling: Beware false friends
Don't be mealy-mouthed; say what you think and do what you want to do

light: Offering or being offered an option
Seeing the value of another person's approach
Understanding there's more than one way to "skin a cat"
Successfully doing more than one thing at a time
Being empowered to make a choice
shadow: Misrepresenting your intentions
Doing one thing while desiring another
Changing course mid-stream for no good reason
Refusing to change your goal even when pursuing it no longer makes sense
Disregarding the input of others

cards.Affirmation: "With my goals in mind, I make confident choices."
Questions to Ask: In a conflict, how do you decide who wins?
What values govern your decision-making process?
What choice will you make if you make no choice at all?
cards.meaning_upright: Planning, future vision, decision, expansion, and the world in hand.
cards.meaning_reversed: Fear of the unknown, playing it safe, poor planning, restlessness, and indecision.
cards.love: Weighing a decision about the relationship's future direction.
cards.career: Strategic planning and considering expansion beyond the current comfort zone.
cards.spiritual: Expanding vision, the soul outgrowing familiar walls, imagining a larger life.
cards.waite_description: A tall man looks from a battlemented roof over sea and shore; he holds a globe in his right hand, while a staff in his left rests on the battlement; another is fixed in a ring. The Rose and Cross and Lily should be noticed on the left side.
cards.waite_divinatory: Between the alternative readings there is no marriage possible; on the one hand, riches, fortune, magnificence; on the other, physical suffering, disease, chagrin, sadness, mortification. The design gives one suggestion; here is a lord overlooking his dominion and alternately contemplating a globe; it looks like the malady, the mortification, the sadness of Alexander amidst the grandeur of this world's wealth.
cards.waite_reversed: Surprise, wonder, enchantment, emotion, trouble, fear.

cards.name: Three of Wands
fortune_telling: You'll be planning a trip soon
Be on the lookout: your ship is coming in

light: Seeing your plans come to fruition
shadow: Procrastinating
Knowing what to do, but refusing to do it
Launching a project without a clear definition of who should do what
Rejecting an opportunity to try something new
Failing to finish what you start

cards.Affirmation: "I take the steps necessary to put my plans in action."
Questions to Ask: How can you make a habit of breaking your habits?
How can you be a decisive leader in this circumstance?
What's your action plan for the next week, month, year, or decade?
cards.meaning_upright: Progress, expansion, foresight, ships coming in, and momentum.
cards.meaning_reversed: Delays, obstacles to plans, limited vision, setbacks, and impatience.
cards.love: A relationship moving forward, sometimes across distance, with patience being rewarded.
cards.career: Early results confirming a good strategy, think bigger and prepare for growth.
cards.spiritual: Faithful patience, what was set in motion is growing even while it is out of sight.
cards.waite_description: A calm, stately personage, with his back turned, looking from a cliff's edge at ships passing over the sea. Three staves are planted in the ground, and he leans slightly on one of them.
cards.waite_divinatory: He symbolizes established strength, enterprise, effort, trade, commerce, discovery; those are his ships, bearing his merchandise, which are sailing over the sea. The card also signifies able co-operation in business, as if the successful merchant prince were looking from his side towards yours with a view to help you.
cards.waite_reversed: The end of troubles, suspension or cessation of adversity, toil and disappointment.

cards.name: Four of Wands
fortune_telling: Someone is watching and evaluating your work
You may get a wedding invitation soon

light: Sharing in a communal sense of achievement and success
Working together toward a common goal
shadow: Keeping your nose to the grindstone
Recognizing good work by demanding more work
Failing to share in a group celebration
Allowing sour grapes to poison your moment in the sun
Refusing to do your part

cards.Affirmation: "My contributions are worthy of celebration."
Questions to Ask: To what extent am I doing my part?
What kind of recognition would be most meaningful?
How might a celebration now impact community morale?
cards.meaning_upright: Celebration, homecoming, milestone, community, and stability.
cards.meaning_reversed: Instability at home, postponed celebration, transition, lack of support, and shaky foundations.
cards.love: Happy milestones, moving in together, engagements, or simply a joyful, secure phase.
cards.career: A completed stage worth celebrating and a supportive, stable team environment.
cards.spiritual: Gratitude in community, pausing to honor how far things have come, together.
cards.waite_description: From the four great staves planted in the foreground there is a great garland suspended; two female figures uplift nosegays; at their side is a bridge over a moat, leading to an old manorial house.
cards.waite_divinatory: They are for once almost on the surface--country life, haven of refuge, a species of domestic harvest-home, repose, concord, harmony, prosperity, peace, and the perfected work of these.
cards.waite_reversed: The meaning remains unaltered; it is prosperity, increase, felicity, beauty, embellishment.

cards.name: Five of Wands
fortune_telling: Prepare for a fight with your best friend
Remember: once you let words loose, you can't take them back

light: Allowing someone to use his or her own methods to get a job done
Opening the floor for discussion or debate
Comparing progress made so far to standards set earlier
shadow: Berating others for their ridiculous opinions
Picking fights
Offering destructive criticism
Baiting people with barbed remarks
Disrupting progress with an endless stream of pointless objections

cards.Affirmation: "I can express dissent in constructive ways."
Questions to Ask: To what extent is your current issue worth fighting for?
What alternatives are there to outright conflict?
What happens in a "fair fight?" How can you keep this fight fair?
cards.meaning_upright: Conflict, competition, friction, clashing egos, and scattered effort.
cards.meaning_reversed: Avoiding conflict, resolution, inner conflict, release of tension, and picking battles.
cards.love: Bickering, rivalry, or outside interference creating unnecessary tension.
cards.career: Competition and disagreement over direction, channel it into healthy rivalry, not chaos.
cards.spiritual: Ego work, noticing the need to win and redirecting that fire into growth instead of noise.
cards.waite_description: A posse of youths, who are brandishing staves, as if in sport or strife. It is mimic warfare, and hereto correspond the
cards.waite_divinatory: Imitation, as, for example, sham fight, but also the strenuous competition and struggle of the search after riches and fortune. In this sense it connects with the battle of life. Hence some attributions say that it is a card of gold, gain, opulence.
cards.waite_reversed: Litigation, disputes, trickery, contradiction.

cards.name: Six of Wands
fortune_telling: Someone is planning a party for you, but not everyone feels so good about your recent success
Watch out for envious friends

light: Being recognized as a capable person
Being cheered on by the crowd
shadow: Being a bad winner
Allowing your achievements to inflate your ego
Looking down on people who seem less capable
Craving to be the center of attention
Giving or receiving insincere praise
Envying the achievements of others

cards.Affirmation: "I value sincere praise."
Questions to Ask: What kind of recognition do I crave? Why?
How freely do I praise the achievements of others?
What happens when the parade is over?
cards.meaning_upright: Victory, recognition, success, confidence, and public acclaim.
cards.meaning_reversed: Private victory, fall from grace, self-doubt, lack of recognition, and ego inflation.
cards.love: Pride in the relationship and mutual admiration, being 'seen' by a partner.
cards.career: Recognition, promotion, or a win that raises your profile.
cards.spiritual: Success with humility, gracious recognition, honoring those who helped.
cards.waite_description: A laurelled horseman bears one staff adorned with a laurel crown; footmen with staves are at his side.
cards.waite_divinatory: The card has been so designed that it can cover several significations; on the surface, it is a victor triumphing, but it is also great news, such as might be carried in state by the King's courier; it is expectation crowned with its own desire, the crown of hope, and so forth.
cards.waite_reversed: Apprehension, fear, as of a victorious enemy at the gate; treachery, disloyalty, as of gates being opened to the enemy; also indefinite delay.

cards.name: Seven of Wands
fortune_telling: Don't be surprised by a personal attack
Prepare to defend yourself or someone you love

light: Refusing to be silenced through fear or intimidation
Continuing a fight against all odds
Defending yourself against physical and emotional attacks
Refusing to put up with abuse
Clinging to your values despite all pressure to abandon them
shadow: Having a chip on your shoulder
Taking unnecessary risks as a means of proving your fearlessness
Looking for an opportunity to take offense
Responding to constructive criticism with defensiveness
Refusing to stand up for yourself and your beliefs

cards.Affirmation: "I am willing to take a stand for what I believe in."
Questions to Ask: When do you feel most threatened? When do you get defensive?
How capable are you of defending yourself?
What kinds of beliefs are worth defending?
cards.meaning_upright: Defense, standing your ground, perseverance, conviction, and holding advantage.
cards.meaning_reversed: Overwhelm, giving up ground, exhaustion, defensiveness, and yielding.
cards.love: Protecting the relationship from outside pressure, or defending personal boundaries within it.
cards.career: Defending your position, ideas, or territory against competition, persistence wins.
cards.spiritual: Conviction, defending what one truly believes, calmly, without needing everyone's agreement.
cards.waite_description: A young man on a craggy eminence brandishing a staff; six other staves are raised towards him from below.
cards.waite_divinatory: It is a card of valour, for, on the surface, six are attacking one, who has, however, the vantage position. On the intellectual plane, it signifies discussion, wordy strife; in business--negotiations, war of trade, barter, competition. It is further a card of success, for the combatant is on the top and his enemies may be unable to reach him.
cards.waite_reversed: Perplexity, embarrassments, anxiety. It is also a caution against indecision.

cards.name: Eight of Wands
fortune_telling: Watch for a surprising letter in the mail
Your whole world is about to be turned on its ear

light: Moving forward with a plan as quickly as possible
Embracing the idea that nothing stays the same forever
Reacting quickly and appropriately to unforeseen problems
shadow: Giving in to panic
Running in circles and screaming
Insisting things must always stay the same
Stirring the pot just to see what will happen
Rushing others
Refusing to re-evaluate a schedule or program, even when it's clearly no longer appropriate

cards.Affirmation: "I adapt quickly to change."
Questions to Ask: How quickly to you adapt to change?
What would your response be to overwhelming, sudden change?
What changes are on your horizon? How well have you prepared for them?
cards.meaning_upright: Swift movement, momentum, news arriving, alignment, and rapid progress.
cards.meaning_reversed: Delays, frustration, scattered energy, missed timing, and slowdown.
cards.love: Fast-moving romance, important messages, or a relationship suddenly gaining momentum.
cards.career: Rapid developments, quick decisions, and news that sets things in motion.
cards.spiritual: Flow, signs arriving quickly and the invitation to move with them rather than overthink.
cards.waite_description: The card represents motion through the immovable-a flight of wands through an open country; but they draw to the term of their course. That which they signify is at hand; it may be even on the threshold.
cards.waite_divinatory: Activity in undertakings, the path of such activity, swiftness, as that of an express messenger; great haste, great hope, speed towards an end which promises assured felicity; generally, that which is on the move; also the arrows of love.
cards.waite_reversed: Arrows of jealousy, internal dispute, stingings of conscience, quarrels; and domestic disputes for persons who are married.

cards.name: Nine of Wands
fortune_telling: Don't relax yet; there's more to come
The test you're facing now is happening for one reason: to show you who your real friends are

light: Sticking with it for the duration
Dragging yourself across the finish line
Picking yourself up by your own bootstraps
Going as far as you can go and being satisfied with your performance
shadow: Making yourself a martyr
Abandoning your post
Giving up at the first sign of opposition
Being prevented from fulfilling an obligation
Failing to be dependable
Refusing to let something go that needs to be released
Beating a dead horse

cards.Affirmation: "When the going gets tough, I stay the course."
Questions to Ask: How do you cope when things get really tough?
When you get low, what encourages you to go on?
At what point should you be able to let this situation go?
cards.meaning_upright: Resilience, last stand, persistence, guardedness, and nearly there.
cards.meaning_reversed: Burnout, paranoia, giving up at the end, rigid defenses, and battle fatigue.
cards.love: Guardedness from past hurt, the walls that protected once may now be keeping love out.
cards.career: Fatigue near the end of a long effort, endurance through this last stretch pays off.
cards.spiritual: Resilience as practice, tending wounds without letting them close the heart before the last step.
cards.waite_description: The figure leans upon his staff and has an expectant look, as if awaiting an enemy. Behind are eight other staves--erect, in orderly disposition, like a palisade.
cards.waite_divinatory: The card signifies strength in opposition. If attacked, the person will meet an onslaught boldly; and his build shews, that he may prove a formidable antagonist. With this main significance there are all its possible adjuncts--delay, suspension, adjournment.
cards.waite_reversed: Obstacles, adversity, calamity.

cards.name: Ten of Wands
fortune_telling: You're worn out
Back off, take a time out, and let someone else handle things for a while

light: Holding your own in extreme circumstances
Coming to the aid of the oppressed
Knowing and being honest about your own limits
Recognizing when you are not well-suited for a particular task
shadow: Taking on more work than you know you can handle
Refusing to say "No" when you're already overloaded
Making a habit of working overtime
Shielding others from facing the consequences of their own poor judgment
Over-extending yourself on a regular basis

cards.Affirmation: "I respect my own limits."
Questions to Ask: How will you know when you reach the end of your rope?
How easily do you say no to new projects and requests?
What projects could you delegate...or eliminate?
cards.meaning_upright: Burden, overload, responsibility, carrying too much, and final effort.
cards.meaning_reversed: Release, delegation, putting burdens down, collapse, and learning to say no.
cards.love: One partner shouldering the relationship's weight alone, rebalancing is needed.
cards.career: Overload and burnout risk, delegate and prioritize before the load drops you.
cards.spiritual: Release, learning that putting down what was never yours to carry is also an act of faith.
cards.waite_description: A man oppressed by the weight of the ten staves which he is carrying.
cards.waite_divinatory: A card of many significances, and some of the readings cannot be harmonized. I set aside that which connects it with honour and good faith. The chief meaning is oppression simply, but it is also fortune, gain, any kind of success, and then it is the oppression of these things. It is also a card of false-seeming, disguise, perfidy. The place which the figure is approaching may suffer from the rods that he carries. Success is stultified if the Nine of Swords follows, and if it is a question of a lawsuit, there will be certain loss.
cards.waite_reversed: Contrarieties, difficulties, intrigues, and their analogies.

cards.name: Page of Wands
fortune_telling: This card represents a young man or woman with a fiery, enthusiastic demeanor, likely born a Cancer, Leo, or Virgo, who wants to start a new relationship with you

light: Being a cheerleader or ardent advocate for your cause
shadow: Basing your entire self-image on what others think
Seizing every new idea that comes your way without question
Habitually discounting input or feedback from others
Being so eager to "do it yourself" that you hinder your own progress

cards.Affirmation: "I can do this."
Questions to Ask: How easily do you admit your own inexperience?
How can you be a better student or employee?
What qualities would make a total beginner's voyage of discovery easier?
cards.meaning_upright: Enthusiasm, exploration, free spirit, exciting news, and curiosity.
cards.meaning_reversed: Scattered energy, hasty starts, boredom, unreliable news, and restlessness.
cards.love: Flirtation, playful energy, or an admirer with an adventurous spirit.
cards.career: A new idea or opportunity worth exploring with fresh eyes and low stakes.
cards.spiritual: Following inspiration, treating every spark of interest as a possible message from the soul.
cards.waite_description: In a scene similar to the former, a young man stands in the act of proclamation. He is unknown but faithful, and his tidings are strange.
cards.waite_divinatory: Dark young man, faithful, a lover, an envoy, a postman. Beside a man, he will bear favourable testimony concerning him. A dangerous rival, if followed by the Page of Cups. Has the chief qualities of his suit. He may signify family intelligence.
cards.waite_reversed: Anecdotes, announcements, evil news. Also indecision and the instability which accompanies it.

cards.name: Knight of Wands
fortune_telling: This card represents a man with a bold, passionate personality, likely born between July 12th and August 11th, who wants to sweep you off your feet

light: Dazzling those around you with your wit and charm
Convincing others of your right to leadership
shadow: Blundering forward with inadequate skill or information
Running roughshod over the feelings of others
Using sex appeal to manipulate others
Forcing your leadership or ideology on others
Beginning many projects without finishing any

cards.Affirmation: "I can lead the way to success."
Questions to Ask: To what extent have you defined your ultimate goal?
What's the fastest way to get the job done? Is this necessarily the best way?
How long has it been since you looked back to see if others really are following your lead?
cards.meaning_upright: Action, adventure, passion, bold pursuit, and charisma.
cards.meaning_reversed: Impulsiveness, recklessness, scattered passion, delays in action, and hot temper.
cards.love: Intense, fast-burning attraction or a charismatic pursuer, thrilling, but test the staying power.
cards.career: Bold moves and fast opportunities, act decisively but don't skip the details.
cards.spiritual: Passion as fuel, honoring the fire while learning to ground it so it warms rather than burns.
cards.waite_description: He is shewn as if upon a journey, armed with a short wand, and although mailed is not on a warlike errand. He is passing mounds or pyramids. The motion of the horse is a key to the character of its rider, and suggests the precipitate mood, or things connected therewith.
cards.waite_divinatory: Departure, absence, flight, emigration. A dark young man, friendly. Change of residence.
cards.waite_reversed: Rupture, division, interruption, discord.

cards.name: Queen of Wands
fortune_telling: This card represents a woman with an attractive, appealing personality, likely born between March 11th and April 20th, who wants to charm you into doing things her way

light: Helping others focus on the issue at hand
Bringing people together, despite their differences
shadow: Being distracted, or using your charms or skills to distract others from the goal
Calling attention to yourself with negative or unhealthy behaviors
Disrupting group activities as a means of feeding your own ego

cards.Affirmation: "I use my influence to promote unity."
Questions to Ask: How attentive am I?
How can I draw people's attention to what we have in common?
To what extent am I able to convince people to do what I want them to do?
cards.meaning_upright: Confidence, warmth, determination, magnetism, and independence.
cards.meaning_reversed: Insecurity, jealousy, demanding nature, burnout, and dimmed confidence.
cards.love: Passionate, self-assured energy, attraction rooted in independence rather than need.
cards.career: Confident leadership and creative drive that inspires others.
cards.spiritual: Authentic radiance, self-expression as generosity, unapologetic shine.
cards.waite_description: The Wands throughout this suit are always in leaf, as it is a suit of life and animation. Emotionally and otherwise, the Queen's personality corresponds to that of the King, but is more magnetic.
cards.waite_divinatory: A dark woman, countrywoman, friendly, chaste, loving, honourable. If the card beside her signifies a man, she is well disposed towards him; if a woman, she is interested in the Querent. Also, love of money, or a certain success in business.
cards.waite_reversed: Good, economical, obliging, serviceable. Signifies also--but in certain positions and in the neighbourhood of other cards tending in such directions--opposition, jealousy, even deceit and infidelity.

cards.name: King of Wands
fortune_telling: This card represents an older man with a commanding, charismatic personality, likely born between November 13th and December 12th, who prefers to give directions and have them followed

light: Putting old things together in new and exciting ways
Using your experience to solve puzzles and problems
Doing what you set out to do
shadow: Using your creativity to get out of honest work
Investing great energy in avoiding responsibility
Boasting about achievements without putting your expertise to practical use
Lording it over others

cards.Affirmation: "I use my authority and experience to get things done faster."
Questions to Ask: How confident a leader am I?
How can I project more confidence?
How can I offer my expertise in ways that inspire others to follow me?
cards.meaning_upright: Visionary leadership, boldness, entrepreneurship, mastery of fire, and inspiration.
cards.meaning_reversed: Arrogance, tyranny, impulsive leadership, unfulfilled vision, and domineering.
cards.love: A confident, protective partner with big plans, generous, though he must share the wheel.
cards.career: Entrepreneurial vision and decisive leadership, think big and commit fully.
cards.spiritual: Purpose embodied, creative fire directed toward a larger vision.
cards.waite_description: The physical and emotional nature to which this card is attributed is dark, ardent, lithe, animated, impassioned, noble. The King uplifts a flowering wand, and wears, like his three correspondences in the remaining suits, what is called a cap of maintenance beneath his crown. He connects with the symbol of the lion, which is emblazoned on the back of his throne.
cards.waite_divinatory: Dark man, friendly, countryman, generally married, honest and conscientious. The card always signifies honesty, and may mean news concerning an unexpected heritage to fall in before very long.
cards.waite_reversed: Good, but severe; austere, yet tolerant.

cards.name: Ace of Pentacles
fortune_telling: Your health will improve
The check you're looking for really is in the mail

light: Outlining a plan for achieving prosperity
Becoming aware of opportunities to improve income or health
Realizing you have everything you need
Appreciating everything the Universe has given you
Receiving the perfect gift at the perfect time
shadow: Indulging in relentless consumerism
Wanting more, no matter how much you have
Obsessing on your account balance
Suffering from hypochondria
Consuming blessings without expressing gratitude
Taking what you want without concern for the needs of others

cards.Numerology: 1 (The Origin: the starting point, the seed, opportunity)
cards.Affirmation: "I am open to and thankful for my blessings."
Questions to Ask: If I made my decision based solely on practical concerns, what would my decision be?
What resources are available to me?
What will be the physical and financial impact of my decisions?
cards.meaning_upright: New opportunity, prosperity, manifestation, solid start, and material gift.
cards.meaning_reversed: Missed opportunity, shaky foundation, scarcity thinking, delayed reward, and poor investment.
cards.love: A relationship with real-world staying power, grounded, stable, and worth investing in.
cards.career: A concrete opportunity, new job, raise, or venture, with solid long-term potential.
cards.spiritual: The seed of abundance, spirit taking root in matter, grounded gratitude.
cards.waite_description: A hand--issuing, as usual, from a cloud--holds up a pentacle.
cards.waite_divinatory: Perfect contentment, felicity, ecstasy; also speedy intelligence; gold.
cards.waite_reversed: The evil side of wealth, bad intelligence; also great riches. In any case it shews prosperity, comfortable material conditions, but whether these are of advantage to the possessor will depend on whether the card is reversed or not.

cards.name: Two of Pentacles
fortune_telling: It's time to balance the budget
Avoid the temptation to spend critical funds on frivolous goods

light: Determining the value of one option over another
Juggling resources to make ends meet
Making difficult choices based on what's best for your body or your bankbook
Asking for a second opinion on health issues
shadow: Engaging in endless price comparison
Putting off a buying decision for fear of finding a slightly better value later on
Buying something without regard for value
Breaking your budget with unnecessary expenses
Engaging in behavior with no regard for how your body or bankbook will be impacted

cards.Affirmation: "Before taking action, I consider costs."
Questions to Ask: What values govern my decisions?
How willing am I to sacrifice a little pleasure now in order to have more pleasure later on?
Given my current situation, which course of action will give me more of what I really need?
cards.meaning_upright: Juggling priorities, adaptability, balance in motion, flexibility, and managing change.
cards.meaning_reversed: Overcommitment, dropped balls, disorganization, financial strain, and overwhelm.
cards.love: Balancing the relationship against life's other demands, presence needs scheduling too.
cards.career: Multitasking and shifting priorities, adaptability works until it becomes overload.
cards.spiritual: Balance as practice, finding rhythm within motion instead of waiting for stillness.
cards.waite_description: A young man, in the act of dancing, has a pentacle in either hand, and they are joined by that endless cord which is like the number 8 reversed.
cards.waite_divinatory: On the one hand it is represented as a card of gaiety, recreation and its connexions, which is the subject of the design; but it is read also as news and messages in writing, as obstacles, agitation, trouble, embroilment.
cards.waite_reversed: Enforced gaiety, simulated enjoyment, literal sense, handwriting, composition, letters of exchange.

cards.name: Three of Pentacles
fortune_telling: A high-dollar contract is in your future
If you work hard, you'll succeed

light: Doing your part in a group project
Delivering exactly what others have asked for
shadow: Pandering to the tastes of others
Failing to deliver what you've promised
Not delivering your best work unless closely supervised
Ignoring or breaking agreements with those who have invested in you
Refusing to do your part
Failing to abide by a clearly-outlined agreement with yourself or others

cards.Affirmation: "My work produces results."
Questions to Ask: How can I get more done?
What's expected of me? How large a role do I play in controlling those expectations?
What's been agreed to? How well has that agreement been followed?
cards.meaning_upright: Teamwork, craftsmanship, skill recognized, collaboration, and building together.
cards.meaning_reversed: Poor teamwork, mediocrity, lack of recognition, misaligned goals, and working alone.
cards.love: The relationship as a joint project, effort, communication, and shared building.
cards.career: Successful teamwork and skill being noticed by people who matter.
cards.spiritual: Craft as devotion, building carefully with others as a quiet form of worship.
cards.waite_description: A sculptor at his work in a monastery. Compare the design which illustrates the Eight of Pentacles. The apprentice or amateur therein has received his reward and is now at work in earnest.
cards.waite_divinatory: Métier , trade, skilled labour; usually, however, regarded as a card of nobility, aristocracy, renown, glory.
cards.waite_reversed: Mediocrity, in work and otherwise, puerility, pettiness, weakness.

cards.name: Four of Pentacles
fortune_telling: A rainy day is coming—it's time to save

light: Fasting as part of a spiritual practice
Dieting in an effort to improve your body
Abstaining from sex as a way of honoring a spiritual tradition or personal promise
shadow: Being stingy
Refusing to spend money that needs to be spent
Withholding sex from your partner
Taking care of your own needs exclusively, without regard for the needs of others
Spending a dollar to save a penny
Failing to be a good manager of the blessings you've been given

cards.Affirmation: "I use my resources wisely."
Questions to Ask: What factors determine how conservative or generous you are?
What kinds of things must be preserved at all costs?
When is greediness or stinginess a good trait to have? When might generosity work against you?
cards.meaning_upright: Security, holding on, control, saving, and possessiveness.
cards.meaning_reversed: Letting go, generosity, loosening control, financial release, and spending.
cards.love: Possessiveness or emotional withholding, safety that comes from control isn't intimacy.
cards.career: Financial caution and consolidation, with a caution against hoarding resources or authority.
cards.spiritual: Loosening attachment, learning that security gripped becomes a cage, and released becomes trust.
cards.waite_description: A crowned figure, having a pentacle over his crown, clasps another with hands and arms; two pentacles are under his feet. He holds to that which he has.
cards.waite_divinatory: The surety of possessions, cleaving to that which one has, gift, legacy, inheritance.
cards.waite_reversed: Suspense, delay, opposition.

cards.name: Five of Pentacles
fortune_telling: Finances are getting tighter
Prepare for a setback

light: Recognizing your needs and taking action to fulfill them
Doing as much as you can do with what little you have
Embracing the aid that comes your way
Focusing on what you have versus what you don't
Looking for the light at the end of the tunnel
shadow: Exaggerating your financial or physical needs
Adopting a poverty mentality
Refusing to support yourself
Refusing offers of support
Playing the martyr
Turning down opportunities to improve your health or finances
Wallowing in misery

cards.Affirmation: "I have faith that what I need will appear."
Questions to Ask: What critical resources do I lack?
What people or groups would come to my aid if I asked?
How might an impoverished spirit be impacting my physical or financial condition?
cards.meaning_upright: Hardship, scarcity, feeling left out in the cold, financial loss, and isolation.
cards.meaning_reversed: Recovery, help accepted, hardship ending, finding shelter, and renewed hope.
cards.love: Feeling shut out, weathering hardship together, letting a partner in from the cold.
cards.career: Financial strain or job insecurity, seek the available support rather than enduring alone.
cards.spiritual: Faith through hardship, remembering the door is unlocked and letting help in is not failure.
cards.waite_description: Two mendicants in a snow-storm pass a lighted casement.
cards.waite_divinatory: The card foretells material trouble above all, whether in the form illustrated--that is, destitution--or otherwise. For some cartomancists, it is a card of love and lovers-wife, husband, friend, mistress; also concordance, affinities. These alternatives cannot be harmonized.
cards.waite_reversed: Disorder, chaos, ruin, discord, profligacy.

cards.name: Six of Pentacles
fortune_telling: When you need help, ask for it
Remember, though: what you receive may be limited by what you've given to others in the past

light: Giving time, money, or effort to a charity
Taking part in a group effort
Lending your resources to others without expecting anything in return
Making sure everyone is treated equally
Working together toward a common goal
shadow: Making a loan as a means of gaining control over someone
Using charitable acts to draw attention to yourself
Dividing work or resources unfairly
Failing to do your part in a group effort
Ignoring obligations and commitments

cards.Affirmation: "Knowing I will receive more, I share my resources freely."
Questions to Ask: How do I feel about charity? About giving it? About receiving it?
How can I know if I'm treating others fairly?
What could I give that no one else can?
cards.meaning_upright: Generosity, giving and receiving, support, fairness, and charity.
cards.meaning_reversed: Strings attached, power imbalance, one-sided giving, debt, and self-neglect in giving.
cards.love: Balance of giving and receiving, circulating care, mutual support.
cards.career: Mentorship, raises, or support networks, generosity now returns multiplied.
cards.spiritual: The sacred flow, abundance circulating, blessing the giver and receiver alike.
cards.waite_description: A person in the guise of a merchant weighs money in a pair of scales and distributes it to the needy and distressed. It is a testimony to his own success in life, as well as to his goodness of heart.
cards.waite_divinatory: Presents, gifts, gratification another account says attention, vigilance now is the accepted time, present prosperity, etc.
cards.waite_reversed: Desire, cupidity, envy, jealousy, illusion.

cards.name: Seven of Pentacles
fortune_telling: Things won't work out as expected
Pick up the pieces and prepare to move on

light: Looking at results with an eye toward improving performance
Coming up with ideas for improving your health or prosperity
Deciding it's time for a change
shadow: Becoming distracted by melancholy thoughts
Longing for "the good old days"
Beating yourself up over lost opportunities
Judging your own work harshly
Holding others to inappropriate standards
Refusing to take part in a project, then whining about the quality of the outcome

cards.Affirmation: "To stay on target, I measure my progress."
Questions to Ask: To what extent have I fulfilled my own expectations?
What are the terms of success?
How can I be happier with the progress I've made?
cards.meaning_upright: Patience, assessment, long-term view, slow growth, and waiting for harvest.
cards.meaning_reversed: Impatience, wasted effort, poor returns, giving up early, and misdirected work.
cards.love: A pause to assess the relationship's growth, investment is judged by trajectory, not just time served.
cards.career: Long-term projects maturing slowly, evaluate returns honestly before investing further.
cards.spiritual: Trust in slow growth, honoring seasons that ripen quietly beneath the surface.
cards.waite_description: A young man, leaning on his staff, looks intently at seven pentacles attached to a clump of greenery on his right; one would say that these were his treasures and that his heart was there.
cards.waite_divinatory: These are exceedingly contradictory; in the main, it is a card of money, business, barter; but one reading gives altercation, quarrels--and another innocence, ingenuity, purgation.
cards.waite_reversed: Cause for anxiety regarding money which it may be proposed to lend.

cards.name: Eight of Pentacles
fortune_telling: Stop over-analyzing, researching, and outlining
Buckle down and get the work done

light: Bringing enthusiasm and zeal to your work
Making an effort to be the best you can be
Finding the work that is right for you
Taking care of the small details
shadow: Working yourself to death
Doing a half-hearted or sloppy job
Continuing in a job you hate
Buying thoughtless gifts
Producing work with shoddy craftsmanship
Rushing through your work
Rejecting opportunities to learn more about your craft

cards.Affirmation: "I give myself wholeheartedly to the task of the moment."
Questions to Ask: How long has it been since you were "lost in your work?"
How can you improve your level of dedication and focus?
What work do you do best? What about that work appeals to you?
cards.meaning_upright: Diligence, skill-building, craftsmanship, dedication, and mastery through repetition.
cards.meaning_reversed: Perfectionism, tedium, cut corners, misplaced effort, and burnout from grind.
cards.love: Connection as craft, small consistent efforts, done attentively, build the bond.
cards.career: Skill development and dedicated work, the mastery being built now compounds.
cards.spiritual: Work as meditation, mastery built one mindful repetition at a time.
cards.waite_description: An artist in stone at his work, which he exhibits in the form of trophies.
cards.waite_divinatory: Work, employment, commission, craftsmanship, skill in craft and business, perhaps in the preparatory stage.
cards.waite_reversed: Voided ambition, vanity, cupidity, exaction, usury. It may also signify the possession of skill, in the sense of the ingenious mind turned to cunning and intrigue.

cards.name: Nine of Pentacles
fortune_telling: Until you appreciate what you have, you won't have any luck getting more

light: Investing time in learning or teaching a difficult task
Restraining yourself from physical or financial extremes
Making sacrifices as a way of achieving larger goals
Breaking a complex task down into simple steps
Knowing the difference between needs and wants
shadow: Being assigned to a task without being trained to perform it
Pursuing a position for which you are not qualified
Disregarding requirements
Refusing to dedicate adequate time or attention when learning about something or someone new
Always craving more

cards.Affirmation: "I know enough to be confident in my decisions."
Questions to Ask: If you could not have what you want, how would you make do?
Could you make a complex task easier by breaking it down into smaller steps?
How patient are you during the learning process? With yourself? With others?
cards.meaning_upright: Self-sufficiency, earned luxury, independence, refinement, and enjoying success.
cards.meaning_reversed: Overdependence, hollow success, overwork without enjoyment, financial insecurity, and self-worth tied to wealth.
cards.love: Wholeness over neediness, a partnership of two complete people, or contentment in one's own company.
cards.career: Financial independence and well-earned rewards, your work stands on its own merit.
cards.spiritual: Gratitude for earned abundance, independence held lightly, savored fully.
cards.waite_description: A woman, with a bird upon her wrist, stands amidst a great abundance of grapevines in the garden of a manorial house. It is a wide domain, suggesting plenty in all things. Possibly it is her own possession and testifies to material well-being.
cards.waite_divinatory: Prudence, safety, success, accomplishment, certitude, discernment.
cards.waite_reversed: Roguery, deception, voided project, bad faith.

cards.name: Ten of Pentacles
fortune_telling: Big money is in the near future
Expect a powerful blessing to come your way

light: Celebrating your physical and financial blessings
Realizing how lucky or how blessed you are
Being satisfied with your physical and financial achievements
Taking best advantage of "times of plenty"
Showering friends or family with gifts
shadow: Spending all of your money on extravagant gifts and possessions
Trying too hard to impress others with your wealth or physique
Giving an inappropriately expensive gift as a means of currying favor
Obsessing on matters of weight, health, or finance
Always asking, "What's in it for me?"

cards.Affirmation: "I keep physical and financial matters in perspective."
Questions to Ask: How much stuff do I really need?
How do I feel about wealth and abundance? How do I define these terms?
How might shedding some possessions open room for growth?
cards.meaning_upright: Legacy, lasting wealth, family security, inheritance, and long-term foundations.
cards.meaning_reversed: Family disputes over resources, unstable foundations, short-term thinking, lost inheritance, and wealth without belonging.
cards.love: Commitment with deep roots, family, shared assets, a future measured in decades.
cards.career: Lasting success, established institutions, and decisions best made on a generational timescale.
cards.spiritual: Legacy, blessings tended so well they outlive the hands that planted them.
cards.waite_description: A man and woman beneath an archway which gives entrance to a house and domain. They are accompanied by a child, who looks curiously at two dogs accosting an ancient personage seated in the foreground. The child's hand is on one of them.
cards.waite_divinatory: Gain, riches; family matters, archives, extraction, the abode of a family.
cards.waite_reversed: Chance, fatality, loss, robbery, games of hazard; sometimes gift, dowry, pension.

cards.name: Page of Pentacles
fortune_telling: This card represents a young man or woman with an earthy, practical demeanor, likely born an Aries, Taurus, or Gemini, who playfully encourages you to take financial or sexual risks

light: Learning the value of a dollar
Taking the first steps toward getting out of debt
Launching a diet, a weight-lifting program, or a health-related effort
shadow: Trying to appear healthier or wealthier than you really are
Spending money carelessly
Living strictly for today, with no thought of tomorrow
Possessing immature attitudes toward sex and sexuality
Using wealth or beauty as an excuse for not having to learn and grow

cards.Affirmation: "I am physically and financially responsible."
Questions to Ask: How can you get more financial or sexual experience without risking your livelihood or health?
How might hands-on learning play a role in your situation?
What's the most practical choice you could make?
cards.meaning_upright: Studiousness, new practical skill, opportunity to learn, grounded ambition, and diligent beginnings.
cards.meaning_reversed: Procrastination, lack of progress, daydreaming without doing, short-lived focus, and missed lessons.
cards.love: Sincere, steady interest that builds slowly and values reliability over fireworks.
cards.career: Learning opportunities, new practical ventures, and ambitions pursued step by step.
cards.spiritual: Grounded manifestation, treating study and small steps as the honest magic they are.
cards.waite_description: A youthful figure, looking intently at the pentacle which hovers over his raised hands. He moves slowly, insensible of that which is about him.
cards.waite_divinatory: Application, study, scholarship, reflection another reading says news, messages and the bringer thereof; also rule, management.
cards.waite_reversed: Prodigality, dissipation, liberality, luxury; unfavourable news.

cards.name: Knight of Pentacles
fortune_telling: A stingy person may chide you for spending money
Be prepared to defend an economic or sexual decision

light: Paying close attention to physical or financial details
Finding creative ways to "make do" with resources on hand
shadow: Throwing caution to the four winds
Spending without regard for consequence
Spending on luxury when necessities are lacking
Escaping stress by spending money
Obsessing on tiny physical or financial details
Perpetually chasing after some new bauble
Copying another's work and claiming it as your own

cards.Affirmation: "I temper my actions with cautious optimism."
Questions to Ask: What's the difference between caution and fear?
How can I evaluate the practicality of my own ideas and methods?
How realistic are my goals?
cards.meaning_upright: Reliability, methodical progress, hard work, patience, and commitment to routine.
cards.meaning_reversed: Stagnation, boredom, stubbornness, workaholism, and stuck in a rut.
cards.love: A dependable partner, devotion through consistency rather than drama.
cards.career: Slow, thorough progress and trustworthy execution, the tortoise wins this one.
cards.spiritual: Devotion through consistency, showing up daily as its own quiet vow.
cards.waite_description: He rides a slow, enduring, heavy horse, to which his own aspect corresponds. He exhibits his symbol, but does not look therein.
cards.waite_divinatory: Utility, serviceableness, interest, responsibility, rectitude-all on the normal and external plane.
cards.waite_reversed: inertia, idleness, repose of that kind, stagnation; also placidity, discouragement, carelessness.

cards.name: Queen of Pentacles
fortune_telling: This card represents a woman with an expansive, sensual nature, likely born between December 13th and 31st, who uses sensual appeal and the promise of reward to sway others to her point of view

light: Appreciating fine food, fine wine, beautiful art, beautiful bodies, or any of the better things in life
Splurging on the occasional "nice to have" item
Rewarding someone with compensation above and beyond expectations
shadow: Indulging in gluttony or greediness
Becoming insatiable
Blunting the impact of treats by indulging in them too often
Providing physical comfort without providing for emotional needs
Allowing a feeling of entitlement to distort your gratitude for what you're given

cards.Affirmation: "I relish the best this world has to offer."
Questions to Ask: How do I define luxury?
To what extent am I capable of reveling in sensual pleasure?
What would I have to give up in order to "have it all?"
cards.meaning_upright: Nurturing practicality, resourcefulness, warmth and security, working provider, and down-to-earth care.
cards.meaning_reversed: Self-neglect, work-home imbalance, smothering through providing, financial codependence, and depleted caregiver.
cards.love: Steady, tangible devotion, a home built together, care shown in daily acts.
cards.career: Resourceful management and creating environments where people and projects flourish.
cards.spiritual: The sacred ordinary, body, home, and hearth tended as holy ground.
cards.waite_description: The face suggests that of a dark woman, whose qualities might be summed up in the idea of greatness of soul; she has also the serious cast of intelligence; she contemplates her symbol and may see worlds therein.
cards.waite_divinatory: Opulence, generosity, magnificence, security, liberty.
cards.waite_reversed: Evil, suspicion, suspense, fear, mistrust.

cards.name: King of Pentacles
fortune_telling: This card represents an older man with a financially, socially, and politically conservative spirit, likely born between August 12th and September 11th, who is known for putting his money where his mouth is

light: Having more than enough to get by
Making contributions to a savings plan
Taking a new job with an eye toward advancing your career
Taking on the role of enforcer when called upon to do so
shadow: Becoming so conservative you resist all change on principle alone
Ignoring innovations in the name of preserving tradition
Being smug or cocky
Becoming ruthlessly dedicated to profit or pleasure
Being sexually selfish
Bossing others around, especially when you're not empowered to do so

cards.Affirmation: "I embody confidence and fairness."
Questions to Ask: How can you handle expenses with greater confidence and maturity?
How dependable are you? How dependable would others say you are?
To what extent is a conservative viewpoint valuable? At what point does it become more of a burden than a blessing?
cards.meaning_upright: Material mastery, abundance, steady leadership, business acumen, and reliable provider.
cards.meaning_reversed: Greed, materialism, rigid control of resources, status obsession, and corruption of comfort.
cards.love: A committed provider, bedrock loyalty, warmth alongside security.
cards.career: The summit of material success, sound investments, business leadership, and enduring prosperity.
cards.spiritual: Stewardship, wealth as responsibility, prosperity shared with gratitude.
cards.waite_description: The figure calls for no special description the face is rather dark, suggesting also courage, but somewhat lethargic in tendency. The bull's head should be noted as a recurrent symbol on the throne. The sign of this suit is represented throughout as engraved or blazoned with the pentagram, typifying the correspondence of the four elements in human nature and that by which they may be governed. In many old Tarot packs this suit stood for current coin, money, deniers. I have not invented the substitution of pentacles and I have no special cause to sustain in respect of the alternative. But the consensus of divinatory meanings is on the side of some change, because the cards do not happen to deal especially with questions of money.
cards.waite_divinatory: Valour, realizing intelligence, business and normal intellectual aptitude, sometimes mathematical gifts and attainments of this kind; success in these paths.
cards.waite_reversed: Vice, weakness, ugliness, perversity, corruption, peril.
